frozen_components: generative retrieval LLM (3B or 1B dense parameters)
architecture.summary: STATIC is a constrained decoding system for LLM-based generative retrieval that flattens prefix trees (tries) into Compressed Sparse Row (CSR) matrices, transforming irregular tree traversals into vectorized sparse matrix operations executable on TPUs/GPUs. The system maintains a transition state vector tracking position in the prefix tree for each beam, uses a dense tensor mask for early decoding layers (up to layer L_d), and applies a Vectorized Node Transition Kernel (VNTK) for deeper layers. Logits are converted to log-probabilities, masked against valid token transitions, and passed to beam search with constrained selection. The architecture is branch-free and fully static, enabling XLA/Inductor compilation without CPU callbacks.

biblio.title: Vectorizing the Trie: Efficient Constrained Decoding for LLM-based Generative Retrieval on Accelerators

claims.dataset: YouTube video recommendation corpus (million constrained vocabulary items)
claims.metric: latency overhead per step; percentage of inference time
claims.statement: STATIC achieves latency overhead of 0.033 ms per decoding step, representing 0.25% of total inference time on a 3 billion parameter model

claims.dataset: YouTube video recommendation (million items, 7-day freshness constraint)
claims.statement: STATIC achieves 948x speedup over a CPU trie implementation

baseline.name: PPV (Parallel Prefix-Verification, binary search on sorted array)

claims.statement: STATIC achieves 47-1033x speedup over hardware-accelerated binary-search baseline (PPV)

claims.metric: memory usage per constraint set size
claims.statement: STATIC requires approximately 0.4 MB of memory for every 1 million restricted vocabulary items; for million-item constraint set, maximal HBM usage is 200 GB (tightenable to 100 GB)

In online A/B test on YouTube Home Feed with 7-day freshness constraint: 7-day fresh video views increased by [value not stated in extracted text], 3-day fresh views increased by [value not stated], click-through rate increased by [value not stated], and strategic user segment satisfaction improved by [value not stated]

baseline.name: PPV and CPU trie (which scale logarithmically or linearly with vocabulary size)

claims.statement: STATIC maintains constant latency with respect to SID vocabulary size across practical ranges (256 to 32k tokens tested)

baseline.name: PPV (binary search), which scales O(log n) in I/O complexity

claims.statement: I/O complexity of STATIC scales linearly with constraint set size, contrasting with logarithmic scaling of binary-search-based methods
STATIC requires precomputing and storing dense tensor masks for early decoding layers; construction becomes prohibitively expensive for layers beyond L_d due to exponential growth of vocabulary space
